@ConfigKey or @ConfigDef Java annotation (i.e., no @interface definition for these). Instead, the 'annotation pattern' refers to the programmatic ConfigDef/ConfigKey configuration documentation pattern, where:\\n\\n1. **Annotation definitions**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java` defines the `ConfigDef` class (with its inner `ConfigKey` class) which serves as the schema/annotation mechanism for all Kafka configuration documentation. `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java` is the base class.\\n\\n2. **Config documentation generators**: `connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java` and `PredicateDoc.java` iterate ConfigDef instances and call `toHtml()` to generate HTML documentation. Here's a summary of what was found:\n\n## Summary\n\nIn Apache Kafka (`sg-evals/kafka--0753c489`), there are no actual Java annotations (`@interface`) named `@ConfigKey` or `@ConfigDef`. Instead, the \"annotation pattern\" refers to the **programmatic `ConfigDef`/`ConfigKey` configuration documentation pattern**:\n\n### 1. Core Framework (annotation definitions)\n- **`ConfigDef.java`** — defines the `ConfigDef` class with its inner `ConfigKey` class; these serve as the configuration schema/annotation mechanism\n- **`AbstractConfig.java`** — base class for all configuration subclasses\n\n### 2. Config Documentation Generators\n- **`TransformationDoc.java`** and **`PredicateDoc.java`** — generate HTML documentation by calling `configDef.toHtml()`\n- Many config classes have `public static void main()` that calls `CONFIG.toHtml(...)` to emit docs: `AdminClientConfig`, `LogConfig`, `GroupConfig`, `DistributedConfig`, `SinkConnectorConfig`, `SourceConnectorConfig`, `MirrorHeartbeatConfig`, `MirrorCheckpointConfig`, `MirrorSourceConfig`, `MirrorConnectorConfig`\n\n### 3.
